passages.text: Hello. This is Min.👋 Want to chat with me? [[ ->The Perspective project]] [[ ->Bobo]] [[ ->Facial]] [[->Web Projects*]] [[Wait..Who are you?->Who are you?]]
passages.name: Hello*

passages.text: Sunset Avenue is a web application project initiated in mid-2023. The concept revolves around crafting an image box integrated with Google Calendar, allowing the display to evolve over time. Personally, the idea is inspired by the daily challenges of communication with my overseas family. It also stems from my longstanding quest to strike a delicate balance between digital and classical art. The project is developed in Node.Js, React, Express, MongoDB, Socket.io [[What did you learn from this project?->learned from sunset]] [[What other web project did you work on?->Web Projects*]]

links.name: What did you learn from this project?
What other web project did you work on?

passages.name: Sunset Ave

passages.text:
This project taught me a great deal, both technically and philosophically. On the technical side, I delved deep into backend development.

From a philosophical standpoint, redesigning the UI multiple times made me ponder the profound impact of design on our perception of objects. Additionally, contemplating the ancient belief in the sun as a god revealed its rationality when considering our dependency on its position for life itself. It's fascinating to think how, as modern humans, we continue to live our lives in sync with the sun each day.
passages.name: learned from sunset

passages.text: I've worked as an professional facial artist in film, game industry. Not only is observing the deformation of flesh and creating emotional expressions enjoyable, but also the communication between the artistic and technical aspects of the work is both fun and rewarding. Initially, I focused solely on facial modeling. However, later on, I expanded my skills to include rigging and Python. I even worked as a facial Technical Director for independent projects. My custom shape editor for freelance work ( ...but I've gained facial wrinkles, unsure if it's natural aging or from using my face as a reference too much. 🧓)
passages.name: Facial

passages.text: This is Bobo the Orangutan. The project I started many years ago as Interactive Artwork Bobo can see you from webcam and follow your expression and play with you. I worked from modeling, texturing, rigging, animation, build the interaction with Computer Vision. If you like this, check out [[Beluga Project]] as well. [[What is the status of Bobo Project?->Why Stopped?]] [[Show me other interactive projects!->Interactive Artwork*]]

links.name: What is the status of Bobo Project?

passages.name: Bobo

passages.text:
<video autoplay control><source src='/media/muse_gorila_short.mp4'></video>

"There is only a perspective seeing, only a perspective "knowing"; and the more affects we allow to speak about one thing, the more eyes, different eyes, we can use to observe one thing, the more complete will our "concept" of this thing, our "objectivity," be."
- Friedrich Nietzsche 

After working on dozens of diverse animal projects, "finally," humans have come into my focus. It's peculiar that my own species is the latest subject, but it feels different now. People who are different from me don't seem annoying anymore; instead, it's astonishing!

The Project Perspectives is an interactive web art exhibit created in 2021. Visitors entering the site can experience different perspectives of characters in the scene, and the space takes on warmth based on who you are observing.

This project emerged as I delved into delivering a distinct experience of sculpture in digital format. It stands out as one of my favorite creations.

<video muted autoplay loop><source src='/media/VR_gorilla.mov'></video>
(It's available in VR as well)

The sculptures were crafted using ZBrush and Blender, while the coding aspect utilized THREE.js and GLSL.

[[Beautiful Idea! How can I try?->Try Perspectives]]
[[What other web project did you work on?->Web Projects*]]

links.name: Beautiful Idea! How can I try?
What other web project did you work on?

passages.name: The Perspective project

passages.text:
... Opting for a grid view is the conventional choice for an artist's portfolio. However, for some reason, it just doesn't align with my purpose.

I believe people anticipate different things from me. Some appreciate my works focused on digital art, while others enjoy my interactive projects in game engines. Some are drawn to my coding projects, and personally, I find sharing my thoughts to be the most rewarding.

In the end, I realized I can't mold myself to fit everyone's expectations, so I decided to embrace it. I created a system where people can customize their page based on their preferences. The page is saved in local browser storage, ensuring that everyone can view my portfolio in their own unique way.

[[How did you make this site?->Making this site?]]

links.name: How did you make this site?

passages.name: This Site

passages.text:
Thank you for saying that❤️‍🔥

[[Back to main->Hello*]]
passages.name: Keep going!

passages.text: Conversation is designed with Twine and React. You can clone the repo and modify as your own.
passages.name: Making this site?

passages.text:
I have but rarely update.
There are two main reasons.

1. Video compression is horrible there.
2. It makes me less productive

I particularly sensed that social media platforms were pushing me towards obsessively posting rather than focusing on improving the quality of my work. I acknowledge that it's not entirely negative, but I am aware of my tendencies. As a result, I've chosen to maintain some distance from social media, even though I miss the productive interactions it offers at times.

If you like talk, [[contact->contact*]] me directly.
passages.name: SNS?

passages.text: Timeline is a straightforward quiz web application crafted in 2023 to aid in the comprehension of the chronological order of historical events. The concept originated from my desire to alleviate the stress associated with studying history. The game's rules and visual design draw significant inspiration from the board game Wavelength. This application is built using NodeJs and React, with the historical database benefiting from ChatGPT's assistance. I manually researched individual events to enhance the accuracy and richness of the historical content. [[What other web project did you work on?->Web Projects*]]

links.name: What other web project did you work on?

passages.name: That Timeline

passages.text: If you ask "what I can", I trained myself as an artist. I can paint, sculpt as natural. [[show me more of your arts ->Drawings]] I've worked in various films and games, animations, application as a digital artist. [[Tell me about your professional experience.->Professional Experience]] In the last few years, I discovered the beauty of programming, landed a full-time developer position. Yet I'm not just my skills; I'm more like a blob of curiosity⭐. passages.name: Who are you?

passages.text:
Thanks for asking! 🥹
After I left the lastest full time job, I traveled and spend time for learning and developing myself. 
In 2023, I've been mostly doing web-development projects. ([[Mupp]], [[Sunset Ave]] , [[That Timeline]] )

Now I am more open to find a opportunity for collaboration.
That's why I renovate my site haha 🤞

[[Lucky you! I have a job for you!->contact*]]

On another note, I'm currently residing somewhere in Vancouver, dedicated to expanding my knowledge and thoughts through reading and studying. Additionally, I've found a philosophical beauty in playing board games, which keeps myself playful .🎉

passages.name: Thesedays*

passages.text:
Mupp is extended project from [[The Perspective project]].

The initial concept is an online museum space that allows interaction with other visitors online simultaneously. It's currently under construction. To be honest, I'm taking a break to refresh my ideas.

<video muted autoplay loop><source src='/media/sequence1.mp4'></video><video muted autoplay loop><source src='/media/muse_gallery.mp4'></video>

passages.name: Professional Experience

passages.text: -> Beluga is one of early interactive animal project. I strategically opted for a whale because 90% of the animation involves the spine, making it simpler than limb animations. A beluga detects a human position through the camera, and procedurally creates the motion per frame. That time, I loved Computer Vision so much. I wanted to connect everything with CV. 😂 ( it continued to [[Bobo]] ) [[Why did you stop Beluga Project?->Why Stopped?]] [[Show me more of the interactive Artworks->Interactive Artwork*]]

links.name: Why did you stop Beluga Project?
Show me more of the interactive Artworks

passages.name: Beluga Project

passages.text:
Time is limited, and I can only do so much in a given period. The longer a project goes on, the more I realize how little I actually know. In each endeavor, I strive for depth rather than surface-level results. This often means taking a step back, studying more, and delving into the intricacies of the work.

[[I love what you do! Please don't give up!->Keep going!]]

links.name: I love what you do! Please don't give up!

passages.name: Why Stopped?

passages.text: About Web project... I was always a big fan of NotionHQ or Miro etc. And some point, I wishing to have more functionality for my self and started learn to create it. At the beggining, I wasn't really interested in learning so much because I was already happy with my python and c# coding ability that time. However when I realize the accessiblity of the creation, I immidiately fell in love. [[ ->Sunset Ave]] [[->That Timeline]] [[->The Perspective project]]

passages.name: AR Projects

passages.text:
Real-time interactive artwork stands out as a unique realm where digital art excels compared to other mediums. I embarked on this journey using Unity3D as my initial platform, gradually expanding to various other platforms and tools. Lately, I've found myself gravitating more towards web browser-friendly interactive art compared to before.

[[Unity3D Projects->Bobo]]
[[AR Projects]] 
[[Web GL Project->The Perspective project]]
[[Generative Art->Generative Art*]]
passages.name: Interactive Artwork*

passages.text:
When I was young, defining myself felt important, but now I see it differently.

The more I learn and broaden my knowledge and experiences, the more I realize how little I know about the world. That's why I prefer to be a curious being rather than obsessing over becoming an expert.

If possible, I wish to work with diverse people and subjects so I can continuously learn from them.
passages.name: I'm a blob

passages.text:
I started learn to code compute shader in unity
<video muted autoplay loop><source srt='/media/agent_05.mp4'></video><video muted autoplay loop><source srt='/media/agent_04.mp4'></video>
That time, I really felt that I need to revisit basic math😓

Later on, I began using THREE.js for [[The Perspective project]] and seized the opportunity to delve into GLSL. This experience made me recognize the powerful capabilities of shader coding.

This is GLSL I created with my drawing.
<video autoplay muted loop><source src='/media/sequence2.mp4'></video>
This developed the idea to create [[Museum of Perspectives->Mupp]]

passages.name: Generative Art*

passages.text: The "Resolution Party" is a small project I developed for the end of the year. The initial concept was to host a virtual party where nobody had to fully commit, and everyone's character could dance through the endless night. It's a metaverse joke project, perfect for introverts. It's created with P5.js and Firebase. There was a recorded video somewhere.. still searching for it🔍
passages.name: P5.js
